text.title: Error-Tracker to Draft-PR Pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Pipeline fires only when event_count ≥ N AND fixability_score ≥ S, both configured in threshold-policy.yaml.
rule.rationale: Unbounded firing floods reviewer queue; thresholds keep signal-to-noise high.

rule.statement: Webhook payload MUST include stack_trace + breadcrumbs + distributed_trace + linked_repo + recent_diffs. Free-text alerts are filtered out.
rule.rationale: Without structured signal the agent guesses; structured input enables deterministic patch proposal.

rule.statement: Agent opens DRAFT PR only; never marks ready for review nor merges. Human reviewer transitions to ready.
rule.rationale: Auto-merge of agent-authored patch is the documented worst failure mode.

rule.statement: Every draft PR MUST include a regression test that reproduces the original exception. Pure patch PRs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Patch without test = no protection against recurrence.

rule.statement: CODEOWNERS file MUST cover the locus; PR auto-requests the right owner.
rule.rationale: Unrouted draft PRs sit; routed ones get triaged.

rule.statement: If tracker doesn't emit structured signal OR repo has no tests OR exception cluster is flaky / intermittent, STOP.
rule.rationale: Pipeline relies on signal quality; skip when quality is too low.
